title: leptactina delagoensis subsp. bussei
intro: Leptactina delagoensis subsp. bussei is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicinal practices, particularly in Africa, for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Leptactina delagoensis subsp. bussei include the treatment of fever, headaches, and skin conditions, as well as its potential use in treating malaria and other infectious diseases. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
